Title: Tissue-preferential recruitment of electron transfer chains for cytochrome P450-catalyzed phenolic biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: S-lignin; sinapoyl esters; seed coat suberin sinapate
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem; leaf; seed; seed coat suberin
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: S-lignin contributes to lignin composition; leaf sinapoyl esters protect against UV irradiation; seed sinapoylcholine is an antinutritive compound
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 5-hydroxylated phenolics including stem S-lignin, leaf sinapoyl esters, seed sinapoyl esters, and seed coat sinapate
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCB5D is the dominant and largely nonredundant CB5 family member required for F5H1-dependent 5-hydroxylated phenolic biosynthesis. 2. Loss of AtCB5D strongly reduced leaf sinapoyl esters and stem S-lignin, and reduced seed sinapoyl esters; quadruple cb5 mutants further depleted seed phenolics. 3. Complementation with AtCB5D, but not other CB5 genes, rescued cb5d defects, indicating strong functional specialization.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome b5 family; ER-resident hemoprotein electron carrier
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broad and high expression across developing seeds and young/mature stem internodes; abundant across leaf, stem, flower, and developing silique
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA and CRISPR mutants; higher-order mutant analysis; complementation with AtC4H promoter; RT-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; reductase assay; yeast whole-cell and microsomal assays; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtC4H promoter, constitutive/phenylpropanoid-associated promoter used for complementation in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption redirects flux toward G-lignin and can slightly increase G-lignin or suberin ferulate; no obvious growth defect in higher-order cb5 mutants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an obligate electron shuttle for F5H1 but not for C4H/C3'H in planta; reduced by both CBR1 and ATR proteins; other CB5 members show little functional redundancy for these traits
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tissue-specific engineering of phenolic profiles; modulation could reduce seed sinapoylcholine while preserving beneficial leaf/stem phenolics if combined with tissue-specific strategies

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed sinapoyl esters; seed coat suberin sinapate; stem S-lignin
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed sinapoylcholine is antinutritive; S-lignin contributes to lignin composition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Primarily supports accumulation of seed sinapoyl esters and seed coat sinapate, with limited contribution to stem S-lignin
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBR1 physically interacts with AtCB5D and reduces AtCB5D more efficiently than ATR2 in vitro. 2. cbr1 mutants showed drastic depletion of seed sinapoyl esters and seed coat sinapate, but only modest reduction in stem S-lignin and little effect on G-lignin. 3. Thus, the NADH-CBR1-CB5 pathway is the dominant route for seed 5-hydroxylated phenolic synthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively high in stem and developing seed
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant phenotyping; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; recombinant protein reductase assay; yeast reconstitution assays; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption strongly lowers seed phenolics and seed coat sinapate; little effect on stem G-lignin
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not directly transfer electrons to P450; functions through CB5D; dominant in seeds likely due to seed expression and NADH availability
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to reduce antinutritive seed sinapoylcholine through seed-targeted manipulation, with potentially limited impact on stem lignification compared with targeting F5H1 or CB5D

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to lignin deposition and composition in stems
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes stem G-lignin and is the dominant reductase supporting stem S-lignin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. atr2 mutants showed persistent reduction in stem G-lignin throughout development and strong reduction in stem S-lignin. 2. ATR2 interacts with and can reduce AtCB5D, but is less efficient than CBR1 in direct CB5D reduction. 3. ATR2 is the principal CPR functioning in stem phenylpropanoid-monolignol P450 reactions, dominating stem S-lignin synthesis via the NADPH-ATR2-CB5 pathway.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in stem, especially senescent internodes; low in developing seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis across developmental stages; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; recombinant protein reductase assay; yeast reconstitution assays; lignin analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption lowers both G- and S-lignin in stems, potentially affecting lignification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dominant CPR in stem; ATR1 shows minimal role in stem lignin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to alter stem lignin composition, though likely with stronger structural tradeoffs than seed-focused targets

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed sinapoyl esters; seed coat sinapate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor/redundant positive effect on seed sinapoyl esters and seed coat sinapate; little effect on stem lignin
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. atr1 mutants showed no obvious stem lignin phenotype. 2. Loss of ATR1 caused only modest reduction in seed sinapoyl esters and seed coat sinapate. 3. ATR1 likely functions redundantly with ATR2 in some seed phenolic processes but is not a major determinant.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low and constitutive across examined tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; recombinant protein assay; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Minor role relative to ATR2 and CBR1 in the studied pathways
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower-priority engineering target due to weak phenotypic effect

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 5-hydroxylated phenolic branch leading to sinapate derivatives and S-lignin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Coniferyl alcohol; coniferaldehyde; ferulate-derived guaiacyl intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 5-hydroxyconiferyl alcohol; 5-hydroxylated phenolic intermediates leading to sinapate derivatives and S-lignin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes benzene ring 5-hydroxylation of guaiacyl/ferulate-related phenylpropanoid intermediates to form 5-hydroxylated precursors for syringyl lignin and sinapate esters
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursors of S-lignin and sinapate esters
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-control step for 5-hydroxylated phenolic synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes synthesis of S-lignin, leaf sinapoyl esters, seed sinapoyl esters, and seed coat sinapate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F5H1 is essential for synthesis of 5-hydroxylated phenolics; fah1 mutants lose seed sinapoyl esters and strongly impair related products. 2. F5H1 strictly requires CB5D-supported electron transfer and can use both NADPH-ATR-CB5 and NADH-CBR-CB5 pathways. 3. Tissue preference differs: ATR2-CB5 dominates in stem S-lignin synthesis, whereas CBR1-CB5 dominates in seeds and seed coat sinapate formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in stem, similar to ATR2, lower in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in yeast reconstitution system
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context and reductant availability (NADPH versus NADH) strongly influence pathway use
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Null mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; yeast whole-cell feeding assays; yeast microsomal assays; coexpression with redox partners
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: S-lignin; sinapoyl esters; seed coat sinapate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Leaf sinapoyl esters protect against UV; seed sinapoylcholine is antinutritive; S-lignin contributes to lignin properties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption shifts carbon flux toward G-type phenolics and strongly depletes beneficial/protective sinapate derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for manipulating S-lignin and sinapate ester content; tissue-specific modulation could reduce antinutritive seed sinapoylcholine while preserving UV-protective leaf phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Uses both NADH- and NADPH-linked electron transfer routes through CB5D, unlike C4H

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core entry step to para-hydroxylated phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid at the para position to form p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies upstream precursor pool for lignin and related phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports G-lignin and general para-hydroxylated phenolic biosynthesis through ATR-dependent electron transfer
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H activity is strongly enhanced by ATR1 or ATR2 alone and does not require CB5D or CBR1. 2. In vitro, C4H uses NADPH but not NADH as reductant source. 3. Genetic data support ATR2 as the principal redox partner for stem G-lignin-associated C4H function.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in yeast reconstitution system; AtC4H promoter used in complementation constructs for CB5 genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dependence on NADPH rather than NADH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast whole-cell feeding assay; yeast microsomal assay; genetic inference from atr2/cbr1/cb5 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to lignin and suberin aromatic precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation of its supporting reductase ATR2 reduces stem G-lignin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for upstream pathway engineering, but likely broad pleiotropic effects due to central phenylpropanoid role
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents conventional P450 behavior using direct CPR electron transfer without obligatory CB5
